Massachusetts · State Licensing Reference

Speech-Language Pathologist
Licensed Speech-Language Pathologist (SLP) Guide

Issued by the Board of Registration of Speech-Language Pathology and Audiology under the Massachusetts Division of Professional Licensure. Data verified for 2026.

Visit Official Board Portal
Fees Board Contact License Types How to Apply Documents Telepractice Reciprocity FAQ
Total Initial Fee
$225
$150 application + $75 license fee
Renewal
$150
Every 2 years
Processing Time
4 to 8 weeks
Subject to board volume
License Duration
2 years
From date of issuance
Board Contact

Board of Registration of Speech-Language Pathology and Audiology

Mailing Address

1000 Washington St., Suite 710, Boston, MA 02118

Physical Address

1000 Washington St., Suite 710, Boston, MA 02118

Phone & Fax

Phone: (617) 701-8600

Fax: (617) 701-8652

Email

dpl.boards@mass.gov

Credential Categories

Massachusetts SLP License Types

Licensed Speech-Language Pathologist

The full credential to independently practice speech-language pathology in Massachusetts.

Temporary SLP License

Issued to SLP graduates completing their Clinical Fellowship.

Application Process

How to Apply for a Massachusetts Licensed Speech-Language Pathologist (SLP)

Earn a Master's Degree in SLP

Complete a master's degree from a CAA-accredited program.

Pass the Praxis Examination

Achieve a passing score on Praxis (test code 5331, score 162).

Complete Clinical Fellowship

Complete 36 weeks of supervised clinical practice (1,260 hours).

Submit Application

Submit application with fees to the Board of Registration.

Required Documents

Application Document Checklist

  • Official graduate transcriptsFrom a CAA-accredited program.
  • Praxis exam score reportScore of 162 or higher.
  • Clinical Fellowship verificationCF completion documentation.
  • Background check (CORI)Massachusetts Criminal Offender Record Information check.
Telepractice

Massachusetts Telepractice Rules for SLPs

Telehealth Practice

Massachusetts permits telepractice for licensed SLPs. Massachusetts is not currently a member of the ASLP Interstate Compact. A valid Massachusetts SLP license is required.

Practice Laws

Provisional Practice & Reciprocity

Clinical Fellowship & Provisional Rules

Massachusetts issues a Temporary SLP License for graduates completing their Clinical Fellowship.

Reciprocity & Endorsement

Massachusetts does not participate in the ASLP Interstate Compact. Out-of-state SLPs must apply for full Massachusetts licensure.

Frequently Asked Questions

Massachusetts SLP FAQ

How much does a Massachusetts SLP license cost?

$225 initial. $150 biennial renewal.

Is Massachusetts part of the ASLP Interstate Compact?

No. Massachusetts has not enacted ASLP-IC legislation as of 2026.

Does Massachusetts allow telepractice for SLPs?

Yes, with a valid Massachusetts license.
